preamble: The vocalization of [l] to [u] is a form of L-vocalization where the lateral approximant shifts to a high back rounded vowel. This is a common historical development in Romance languages.

examples.note: Syllable-final /l/ vocalized to a high back glide/vowel in most dialects.
Latin 'alba' (white) > Old French 'albe' > Modern French 'aube' [ob] via [au] stage.

sources: Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
